stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In the early 1900s, labor activists organized large strikes for better working conditions.
  • Workers in Lawrence, Massachusetts, went on strike at the textile mills.
  • This strike began on January 12, 1912, and ended on March 14, 1912.
  • Workers in Paterson, New Jersey, went on strike at the silk mills.
  • This strike began on February 25, 1913, and ended on July 28, 1913.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the order in which the two strikes occurred. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Lawrence strike began on January 12, 1912, and ended on March 14, 1912.
Workers in Lawrence, Massachusetts, went on strike at the textile mills, and workers in Paterson, New Jersey, went on strike at the silk mills.
Workers in Paterson, New Jersey, went on a long strike at the silk mills from February 25, 1913, to July 28, 1913.
The Lawrence textile mill strike, which began on January 12, 1912, occurred before the Paterson silk mill strike, which began on February 25, 1913.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the order in which the two strikes occurred by explicitly stating that the Lawrence strike occurred before the Paterson strike, citing the start dates from the notes.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes only the Lawrence strike’s dates, so it cannot convey the order of the two strikes. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence lists both strikes but uses no ordering language. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes only the Paterson strike’s dates, so it cannot convey the order of the two strikes.
